headline: The story behind the edit
bodyMarkdown:
The Lean Kitchen Edit started the way most useful things do: out of frustration. Every "healthy" recipe seemed to demand ingredients from three different specialty stores, or came wrapped in guilt about what you "shouldn't" be eating. We wanted something simpler — real food, realistic effort, no lecture.

## Who this is for

If you've tried diets that felt restrictive, don't have a spare hour to cook most nights, and just want food that's simple, tasty, and doesn't require a nutrition degree to understand — you're exactly who we're writing for.

## What "the edit" means

Every recipe and plan on this site is curated, not just collected. We test for realistic grocery lists, exact measurements, and clear steps — so what you find here is the edited-down, no-fluff version of "eating healthier," not one more overwhelming list of rules.

## Our approach

We publish estimated calorie and macro counts, clearly labeled as estimates, and we never promise a specific result. Nutrition and weight loss aren't one-size-fits-all, so we focus on giving you real options and letting you build the routine that fits your life.
